title: Lesson 1 Overview
subtitle: Listen to the lesson explained in English

transcript.en: Welcome! 👋 Lesson 1. Your first dive into Maltese.
Four things today. **Saying hello**. The **alphabet**. A tiny but important word — **the**. And the **days of the week**. Three minutes. Let's go!
Start simple. To say 'hi' you can say:
Or — first thing in the morning:
To tell someone your name, you say:
Five phrases. That's all you need to start a conversation in Malta. ☕
Quick fact: Maltese has **30 letters**. Most are familiar — same as English. But **five are unique** to Maltese.
Sounds like the **'ch'** in 'chair'. As in:
Sounds like the **'j'** in 'judge'. As in:
A strong English **'h'** — really push the air. As in:
Voiced like the **'z'** in 'zoo'. As in:
And finally — a digraph. Two letters, one sound:
Mostly silent — but it stretches the vowel next to it. ✨
Maltese is **mostly phonetic** — words are written close to how they sound. Spelling is way easier than English. 🎯
Here's where it gets fun. Maltese has **three forms** of the word 'the'. Which one? Depends on the next letter.
RULE 2: vowels, h, or għ — use:
RULE 3: nine special letters — the **sun consonants** — make 'the' **assimilate**. The L copies the next letter.
The nine sun consonants: **ċ, d, n, r, s, t, x, ż, z**. 🌞
Don't memorise the rules right now. The exercises drill it into you the easy way. Your **ears** learn faster than your brain. 👂
Listen and notice the article changing each time. 🎧
Notice — **It-, L-, Il-, Is-** — every rule from Part 3 in action!
Five phrases. 30 letters. Three forms of 'the'. Seven days. That's lesson 1.
Tap the section that interests you most and the cards do the rest. You've got this. 💪
